name: Agriculture, Aquaculture and Fisheries
blurb: Supports the sustainable growth of New Brunswick's agriculture, aquaculture and fisheries sectors — from farm business development and crop insurance to aquaculture licensing and seafood processing.

name: Education and Early Childhood Development
blurb: Leads New Brunswick's anglophone and francophone K-12 school systems and the early learning and child care sector, supporting children from birth through Grade 12.

name: Energy
blurb: Leads provincial energy policy — reliable and affordable electricity, energy efficiency, renewables, and clean energy development including nuclear innovation.

name: Environment and Local Government
blurb: Protects New Brunswick's environment through assessments, approvals and monitoring, leads climate change action, and supports strong, accountable local governments.

name: Finance and Treasury Board
blurb: Manages the province's finances — the budget, taxation, borrowing, and treasury — and oversees the public service as employer.

name: Health
blurb: Funds and oversees New Brunswick's health system — Medicare coverage, hospitals delivered through the Horizon and Vitalité health networks, public health, and mental health and addiction services.

name: Indigenous Affairs
blurb: Leads the province's relationships with First Nations in New Brunswick — coordinating consultation, negotiating agreements, and advancing reconciliation across government.

name: Justice and Public Safety
blurb: Supports the courts and justice services, keeps communities safe through policing oversight, corrections, and emergency management, and regulates activities from liquor sales to private security.

name: Natural Resources
blurb: Manages New Brunswick's Crown lands, forests, fish and wildlife, and mineral resources — half the province is Crown land held in trust for its people.

name: Post-Secondary Education, Training and Labour
blurb: Helps people get the education and skills for good jobs — student financial assistance, apprenticeship, employment services, employment standards, and immigration and settlement.

name: Social Development
blurb: Supports New Brunswickers who need help most — income assistance, affordable housing, child protection, disability supports, and long-term care for seniors.

name: Tourism, Heritage and Culture
blurb: Grows New Brunswick's tourism economy, operates provincial parks and attractions, and supports arts, culture, heritage, sport, and the provincial archives.

name: Transportation and Infrastructure
blurb: Builds and maintains New Brunswick's highways, bridges, and ferries, and constructs and manages public buildings including schools and hospitals.

name: Service New Brunswick
blurb: The government's service delivery organization — one-stop access to licences, registrations, vital statistics, property assessment, and hundreds of other services online, by phone, and in service centres.

name: Opportunities New Brunswick
blurb: The province's economic development agency — helps businesses start, grow, export, and invest in New Brunswick through financing, advice, and investment attraction.

name: Executive Council Office
blurb: Supports the Premier and Cabinet, coordinates government-wide policy and intergovernmental relations, and oversees openness obligations including right to information.

name: Office of Women and Gender Equity
blurb: Advances gender equality in New Brunswick — supporting violence prevention, pay equity, and organizations working for women and gender-diverse people.
